$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to artisanpack namespace; attributes/supports verbatim.

TypeScript port. Omits the blob-URL upload mount-effect (useUploadMediaFromBlobURL), the URL → embed-block upgrade (createUpgradedEmbedBlock), the shared Caption component, the PosterImage helper, and the TracksEditor toolbar dropdown — each depends on block-library internals that are not exposed via the package's exports field. Replaced with inline RichText figcaption + inline MediaUpload for the poster. The tracks attribute still round-trips through edit/save.
TypeScript port; behaviour byte-equivalent to upstream. Tracks are rendered via the vendored sibling tracks.tsx.
Single deprecation entry ported verbatim (figcaption className upgrade).
Upstream files + shortcode + raw transforms preserved; added bidirectional core/video ↔ artisanpack/video block transforms.
Sass caption-style() mixin and base-styles colors/variables/z-index inlined as plain CSS.

Sibling renderer used by both edit.tsx and save.tsx; body logic is byte-equivalent to upstream. The fork adds a JSDoc file-header pointing at the upstream source so the lineage is visible without consulting this state file. No semantic drift from upstream.
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract; upstream init.js / index.php server-side wiring is intentionally absent.

vendoredPrimitives.name: tracks.tsx
vendoredPrimitives.reason: Used by both edit and save to keep editor preview and saved markup in lockstep.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx omits the upstream blob-URL upload mount-effect (useUploadMediaFromBlobURL). Practical impact: drag-and-dropped video files are not eagerly uploaded as soon as they hit the block; the user uploads via MediaPlaceholder or MediaReplaceFlow as usual.

edit.tsx omits the video-URL → embed-block upgrade (createUpgradedEmbedBlock). Practical impact: pasting a YouTube/Vimeo URL into the video URL field does not auto-convert to an embed block; users insert the embed block directly.

edit.tsx uses an inline RichText figcaption instead of the upstream shared Caption component. Practical impact: the toolbar caption-toggle affordance Caption provides is replaced by always-showing the figcaption when selected or non-empty.

edit.tsx replaces the upstream PosterImage helper with an inline MediaUpload button in the settings ToolsPanel. Practical impact: no preview thumbnail of the chosen poster in the sidebar — only a 'replace poster image' button.

edit.tsx omits the TracksEditor toolbar dropdown (it depends on the block-library `lock-unlock` private API). Practical impact: text-track (.vtt) management UI is unavailable in the fork; the `tracks` attribute itself still round-trips losslessly through save/edit so tracks defined via core/video transforms persist.
